concept: x86-64 assembly (NASM syntax, Linux): RDTSC entropy, decimal rendering, write/exit syscalls.
evidence: Intel 64 and IA-32 Architectures Software Developer's Manual — RDTSC, DIV
System V AMD64 ABI and the Linux x86-64 syscall table — write (1), exit (60)
facts: ; assembled by the DSCE polyglot assembler (x86-64 NASM, Linux)

section .bss
buf: resb 4

section .text
global _start
_start:
    rdtsc                       ; time-stamp counter -> EDX:EAX
    xor edx, edx
    mov ecx, 100
    div ecx                     ; EDX = EAX mod 100
    lea eax, [edx + 1]          ; 1..100
    ; render EAX as decimal, newline-terminated, right-aligned in buf
    lea rsi, [rel buf + 3]
    mov byte [rsi], 10          ; newline
    mov ebx, 10
.digit:
    dec rsi
    xor edx, edx
    div ebx
    add dl, '0'
    mov [rsi], dl
    test eax, eax
    jnz .digit
    ; write(1, rsi, end - rsi)
    lea rdx, [rel buf + 4]
    sub rdx, rsi
    mov rax, 1                  ; SYS_write
    mov rdi, 1                  ; stdout
    syscall
    mov rax, 60                 ; SYS_exit
    xor rdi, rdi
    syscall

section .text
global _start
_start:
    mov rax, 60                 ; SYS_exit
    xor rdi, rdi
    syscall

section .text
global _start
_start:
    mov rax, 60                 ; SYS_exit
    xor rdi, rdi
    syscall
